THESE MATERIALS FOCUS ON A WIDE VARIETY OF TOPICS SUCH AS TEHCNOLOGICAL ADVANCEMENTS, MARKETING, INSURANCE, HUMAN RESOURCES, PRODUCTION, CHALLENGES, BEST PRACTICES, AND FINANCIAL MANAGEMENT, AMONG OTHERS. FUNDS WERE ALLOCATED FROM THE CONFERENCE PAYMENTS RECEIVED FOR THE LITTLE ROCK ARKANSAS CONFERENCE HELD 22-25 MARCH 2025. ActivityOrMissionDesc: AS AN ORGANIZATION WE ARE DRIVEN BY THE BELIEF THAT EDUCATION SHOULD BE ACCESSIBLE TO ALL. WE WORK TIRELESSLY TO ELIMINATE BARRIERS TO LEARNING BY OFFERING OUR EDUCATIONAL PROGRAMS FREE OF CHARGE ENSURING THAT OPTOMETRISTS REGARDLESS OF THEIR GEOGRAPHICAL LOCATION OR FINANCIAL MEANS CAN ACCESS HIGH-QUALITY EYE CARE EDUCATION.
MissionDesc: EYE CARE PROFESSIONAL EDUCATION INC IS A 501-C3 NON PROFIT ORGANIZATION COMMITTED TO PROVIDED HIGH QUALITY EDUCATION TO EYE CARE PRACTITIONERS RESIDENTS AND STUDENTS ALL OVER THE WORLD. MANY STUDENTS AND PHYSICIANS ARE UNABLE TO TRAVEL FOR EDUCATION WHICH IS WHY OUR ORGANIZATION DEVELOPS HIGH QUALITY VIRTUAL EVENTS. OUR EVENTS ARE MOSTLY VIRTUAL WHICH ENABLES EYE CARE PROFESSIONALS FROM ANYWHERE TO ACCESS HIGHLY REQUESTED TOPICS PRESENTED BY EXPERTS IN THE EYE CARE FIELD.
Desc: DRY EYE IN THE DESERT This hybrid educational event focuses exclusively on the critical topic of dry eye disease management. Through unique courses and a distinguished panel of industry-leading experts Dry Eye in the Desert educates eye care professionals ECPs on the latest diagnostics and therapeutic strategies. In-person attendees benefit from hands-on skills transfers live presentations and focused interactions with industry sponsors in a dedicated exhibit hall directly contributing to the immediate improvement of clinical practices within the dry eye arena.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: Glaukos ILINK AND COE Keratoconus characterized by the thinning and bulging of the cornea requires precise diagnosis and timely intervention to ensure effective management and improved patient outcomes. The Glaukos Centers of Excellence COE and iLink Users Group ILUG were designed to build advanced knowledge and expertise among practicioners performing corneal cross-linking for Keratoconus patients. This program focuses on in-depth training and resources that empower doctors to deliver exceptional care particularly for complex keratoconus cases. By enhancing doctor proficiency and confidence in this critical treatment the ILUG and COE programs help bring corneal cross-linking into broader practice allowing more
ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp_Desc: LOAN FROM INTERESTED PERSON LOAN WAS PAID IN FULL - NO LOAN AFTER 2024
ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp_Desc: ORAL PHARMACEUTICALS This program delivers a comprehensive interactive curriculum designed to significantly enhance the clinical knowledge of eye care professionals regarding the safe and effective use of oral and systemic pharmacological agents within a primary care optometric practice. The course series provides an in-depth examination of appropriate oral pharmaceutical management for a wide variety of anterior segment and ocular surface diseases including infections inflammatory conditions and special populations such as pediatric patients. The program emphasizes practical clinical application discussing critical information such as systemic dosing identification of adverse allergic reactions use during pregnancy and the crucial evolving landscape of pharmacological agents.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: THE RAPID CITY FINE ARTS COUNCIL'S MISSIONS IS TO CHAMPION THE POWER OF ART TO BRING ALL KINDS OF PEOPLE TOGETHER, MAKE ART MORE ACCESSIBLE, MORE INTEGRAL AND MORE INSPIRING FOR THE COMMUNITIES SERVED.
MissionDesc: THE RAPID CITY FINE ARTS COUNCIL'S MISSIONS IS TO CHAMPION THE POWER OF ART TO BRING ALL KINDS OF PEOPLE TOGETHER, MAKE ART MORE ACCESSIBLE, MORE INTEGRAL AND MORE INSPIRING FOR THE COMMUNITIES SERVED.
Desc: ARTS EDUCATION, EXHIBITS, EVENTS AND INFRASTRUCTURE: THE RAPID CITY FINE ARTS COUNCIL IS CHARGED, THROUGH CITY ORDINANCE, TO BE THE ARTS ADVISOR TO THE CITY OF RAPID CITY. THE ARTS COUNCIL WORKS TO LEVERAGE THE ARTS TO PROVIDE ECONOMIC AND SOCIAL BENEFITS TO PEOPLE IN THE BLACK HILLS REGION THROUGH VARIOUS AVENUES. THE ARTS COUNCIL MAINTAINS A PERMANENT COLLECTION AND PRODUCES PROFESSIONALLY CURATED EXHIBITS OF REGIONAL STATURE, AND BOTH ARE FREE TO THE PUBLIC FOR VIEWING. THE ARTS COUNCIL PROVIDES GALLERY EXPERIENCES FOR CHILDREN THROUGH VARIOUS PROGRAMMING AND OFFERS A VARIETY OF ART CLASSES AND CAMPS FOR ALL AGES. THE ARTS COUNCIL PROVIDES PERFORMING ARTS OPPORTUNITIES BY PRODUCING SHOWS AND FESTIVALS THAT ALLOWS PEOPLE IN THE AREA TO PARTICIPATE AND/OR WATCH.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: FACILITY MANAGEMENT AND MAINTENANCE: THE RAPID CITY ARTS COUNCIL MANAGES THE DAHL ARTS CENTER THROUGH A CONTRACT WITH THE CITY OF RAPID CITY. THE ARTS COUNCIL MANAGES THE BUILDING THROUGH PROACTIVE MANAGEMENT AND PREVENATIVE MAINTENANCE SCHEDULES. INDIVIDUALS, BUSINESSES AND OTHER NON-PROFITS USE THE BUILDING FOR VARIOUS FUNCTIONS AND EVENTS. THESE FUNCTIONS AND EVENTS ARE SCHEDULED AND OVERSEEN BY THE ARTS COUNCIL.

Desc: Youth and Sibling Service program: At Candlelighters we understand that the diagnosis of a child effects everyone in the family. Siblings often find themselves left out when great focus is on their ill sibling. Routines such as carpooling to sports or other extra curricular activities are often suddenly cut due to the familys new normal of the need for one or both parents to be present at constant clinic visits and or hospitalizations. Our programs aim to provide normalizing activities for all children in the family and to provide opportunities for peer support with other siblings experiencing similar circumstances

ActivityOrMissionDesc: To advance, support and encourage the medical practice of phlebology by providing financial and technical resources.
MissionDesc: To advance, support and encourage the medical practice of phlebology by providing financial and technical resources.
Desc: Through the organizations charitable grant making, the field of vein care works with other medical associations to develop standards of care, certificate exams, and educational materials for the physicians and allied healthcare professionals interested in venous and lymphatic disease. It also allows them to have a direct voice on the floor of the AMA and in Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services for recommendations to protect patient access to care.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: Education: The organization has provided funding through grants to an accredited society so that in the evolving world of vein care, it is able to provide the latest training and continuing education to vein care practitioners through live, enduring, and immersive virtual courses. This training to individuals allows them to improve and increase their capabilities when working with their patients for a better quality of life.
ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp_Desc: Research: By conducting practice-based research or practical research based on data collected from our physician members, the organization funds organizations committed to expanding the field of venous and lymphatic research to determine the best strategies and queries for the research. The data collected and analyzed is then used to help improve patient outcomes.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: MINNESOTA TECH FOR SUCCESS CREATES DIGITAL EQUITY FOR STUDENTS BY PARTNERING WITH SCHOOLS AND EDUCATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS TO PROVIDE TECHNOLOGY ACCESS, ENGAGING STEM PROGRAMMING, AND IT WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES.
MissionDesc: MINNESOTA TECH FOR SUCCESS CREATES DIGITAL EQUITY FOR STUDENTS BY PARTNERING WITH SCHOOLS AND EDUCATIONAL ORGANIZATIONS TO PROVIDE TECHNOLOGY ACCESS, ENGAGING STEM PROGRAMMING, AND IT WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT IN UNDERSERVED COMMUNITIES.
Desc: THE PROGRAM INVOLVES REFURBISHING AND RECYCLING COMPUTERS THAT ARE DONATED BY LOCAL CORPORATIONS AND GOVERNMENT AGENCIES. THESE COMPUTERS ARE THEN SOLD AT DISCOUNTED PRICES OR GIFTED TO SCHOOLS, TEACHERS, NOT-FOR-PROFIT ORGANIZATIONS, AND DISADVANTAGED YOUTH THROUGHOUT MINNESOTA. MTFS IMPROVES STUDENT SUCCESS BY TRANSFORMING COMPUTER TECHNOLOGY TO PROVIDE GREATER EDUCATIONAL OPPORTUNITIES FOR STUDENTS IN MINNESOTA. IN 2024, OUR COMPUTER REFURBISHING PROGRAM WAS ABLE TO FILL A CRUCIAL COMMUNITY NEED, REFURBISHING 14,033 COMPUTERS; RECYCLING 19,330 USED TECHNOLOGY ITEMS; AND PLACING 2,274 REFURBISHED COMPUTERS WITH MINNESOTA STUDENTS AND SCHOOLS.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT - IN 2024 WE TAUGHT 314 STUDENTS COMPUTER REFURBISHING SKILLS, COMPTIA TECH+, PROJECT+, AND DATA+. WE ALSO TAUGHT DIGITAL LITERACY CLASSES IN ESSENTIAL COMPUTER SKILLS AND MICROSOFT OFFICE ESSENTIALS. ADDITIONALLY, YOUTH PROGRAMMING INCLUDED CODING CLASSES IN HTML5 AND CSS THROUGH MTFS LEARN TRAINING PROGRAM. CLASSES WERE HELD BOTH IN PERSON AND VIRTUALLY.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: To engage all Northern Virginia communities in enjoying, conserving and restoring nature for the benefit of birds, other wildlife, and people. This is advanced through educational programs, citizen science, conservation initiatives and advocacy initiatives.
MissionDesc: To engage all Northern Virginia communities in enjoying, conserving and restoring nature for the benefit of birds, other wildlife, and people. This is advanced through educational programs, citizen science, conservation initiatives and advocacy initiatives.
Desc: Member services: Communicated and informed members via our newsletter, website, and social media. Conducted outreach at environmental events. Undertook advocacy work, including public education and issue engagement, on statewide, regional, and local issues around threats to wildlife and their habitat
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: Wildlife Sanctuary: Visited 234 sites and certified an additional 53 properties totaling 337 acres, bringing the total acreage under the Wildlife Sanctuary Program to 8,646 acres spread across Northern VA since 2005. Co-sponsored a landscapers conference in Spanish to provide information about caring for native plants and plant communities to 78 participants. Wildlife Sanctuary Ambassadors gave 30 presentations and participated in dozens of fairs, environmental expos, garden club and homeowner association gatherings, and tabling events across the region. A grant from Fairfax Countys Tree Planting and Preservation Fund supported mini-grants of $3,000 to hire landscaping contractors to eliminate invasive vines that threaten trees in HOA common areas or on faith community properties, with a $1,500 match in funds or volunteer labor required. We received 28 applications and awarded 11 grants, three to faith communities and eight to HOAs.
ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp_Desc: Conservation: Coordinated with partner government and conservation organizations to conserve and increase high quality habitat through our Stretch Our Parks program. Provided key tools to reduce non-native invasive plants from critical protected areas on public lands. Continued to conduct wildlife surveys and publish results, providing critical data for long-term trend analysis. Conducted nature walks to educate and engage the community in our conversation efforts.
ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp_Desc: Education: Offered 43 online adult programming and courses on birds, conservation, and natural history to 2,750 participants including NVBA members and the general public. Conducted in-person educational programs, including some with live birds. Sponsored local, regional, and international field trips to birdwatching hotspots. Provided leaders and guest speakers for school field trips, lectures, and home-schooling activities. Trained more than 50 environmental advocates.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD IS A NONPROFIT DEDICATED TO FUNDRAISING FOR RESEARCH INTO THE DEVELOPMENT OF AN EFFECTIVE TREATMENT OR CURE FOR AN ULTRA RARE GENETIC DISORDER: MULTICENTRIC CARPOTARSAL OSTEOLYSIS (MCTO)
MissionDesc: SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD IS A NONPROFIT DEDICATED TO FUNDRAISING FOR RESEARCH INTO THE DEVELOPMENT OF AN EFFECTIVE TREATMENT OR CURE FOR AN ULTRA RARE GENETIC DISORDER: MULTICENTRIC CARPOTARSAL OSTEOLYSIS (MCTO)
Desc: DURING THE FISCAL YEAR ENDING APRIL 2025, SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD FUNDED MAJOR RESEARCH INITIATIVES TO ADVANCE UNDERSTANDING AND TREATMENT OF MULTICENTRIC CARPO-TARSAL OSTEOLYSIS (MCTO) AN ULTRA RARE GENETIC BONE AND KIDNEY DISORDER. 1.ADVANCING MCTO DISEASE MECHANISMS THROUGH INNOVATIVE MOUSE MODELS SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD SUPPORTED RESEARCH AT HARVARD SCHOOL OF DENTAL MEDICINE INVESTIGATING BONE REMODELING ACTIVITY AND CELLULAR CHANGES MAFB MOUSE MODELS OF MCTO. THIS WORK INCLUDES TRANSCRIPTOMIC ANALYSES OF CELLS AND TISSUES WITHIN THE SKELETAL MICROENVIRONMENT TO UNCOVER MOLECULAR MECHANISMS DRIVING DISEASE PROGRESSION. 2.EXPANDING STEM CELL-BASED AND DEVELOPMENTAL MODELS OF MCTO IN COLLABORATION WITH BOSTON CHILDREN'S HOSPITAL, SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD CONTINUED TO FUND STUDIES ON MAFB AND DEVELOPMENTAL CHONDROGENESIS. THESE EFFORTS ADVANCE STEM CELL-BASED MODELS OF MCTO AND IDENTIFY KEY TRANSCRIPTIONAL NETWORKS INVOLVED IN BONE AND CARTILAGE DEVELOPMENT. 3.ESTABLISHING CRITICAL RESEARCH INFRASTRUCTURE FOR RARE DISEASE DISCOVERY SOPHIE'S NEIGHBORHOOD SUPPORTED THE CREATION AND ONGOING MAINTENANCE OF MCTO MOUSE LINES WITH SPECIFIC MAFB MUTATIONS AT THE JACKSON LABORATORY. THESE SPECIALIZED MODELS ENABLE RESEARCHERS TO TEST HYPOTHESES RELATED TO MAFB PROTEIN ACCUMULATION AND DISEASE PATHOLOGY, STRENGTHENING THE FOUNDATION FOR FUTURE THERAPEUTIC RESEARCH. 4.INITIATE ANTISENSE OLIGONUCLEOTIDE (ASO) DEVELOPMENT AND SCREENING TO EVALUATE THERAPEUTIC EFFICACY IN CELLULAR AND MOUSE MODELS, WITH THE GOAL OF REVERSING BONE AND KIDNEY PHENOTYPES ASSOCIATED WITH MCTO.

Building on that spark, our Dollhouse Mentoring Program serves girls ages 1418 through structured mentoring, leadership development, life-skills workshops, workforce and college readiness, and positive female role modeling. We also engage families and community partners to reinforce support systems around each participant. Together, these activities address social-emotional growth, resilience, and long-term success, turning imagination into action and potential into measurable ou

Desc: During the fiscal year, the organization expended $163,442 of a $250,000 state grant award to support mentoring, leadership development, facilities, travel, training, and program materials for adolescent girls in underserved communities. The remaining $86,558 was approved by the grantor to be carried forward and reallocated under a subsequent fiscal year award to continue program delivery.

OUR STUDENTS GAIN A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF THEMSELVES AND ARE INSPIRED TO ACT WITH COMPASSION AND INTEGRITY.
MissionDesc: FRIEND'S SCHOOL IS A SUPPORTIVE, DYNAMIC COMMUNITY COMMITTED TO EDUCATING THE WHOLE CHILD HEAD, HAND, AND HEART AND TO TRAINING EXCEPTIONAL TEACHERS FOR TOMORROW'S CLASSROOMS. WE PROVIDE A STRONG ACADEMIC FOUNDATION WHILE DEVELOPING CREATIVE EXPRESSION AND NURTURING A PASSION FOR LEARNING. OUR STUDENTS GAIN A DEEP UNDERSTANDING OF THEMSELVES AND ARE INSPIRED TO ACT WITH COMPASSION AND INTEGRITY.
Desc: EDUCATIONAL - FRIENDS SCHOOL OFFERS AN INDEPENDENT, NONSECTARIAN PROGRESSIVE PROGRAM FOR CHILDREN IN PRESCHOOL THROUGH GRADE EIGHT. WE ARE LEADERS IN SOCIAL AND EMOTIONAL LEARNING WITH A FOCUS ON MINDFULNESS PRACTICES AT ALL LEVELS. STUDENTS ARE CHALLENGED THROUGH EXPERIENTIAL LEARNING, EXPLORATION AND DIRECT INSTRUCTION, LOW STUDENT TO TEACHER RATIOS, AND ACTIVELY INVOLVED FAMILIES. OUR PRESCHOOL OFFERS A WARM, LOVING, PLAY-BASED APPROACH, SETTING THE FOUNDATION FOR SCHOOL READINESS. IN OUR ELEMENTARY SCHOOL WE EMPHASIZE AN EXPERIENTIAL AND GOLD STANDARD PROJECT-BASED CURRICULUM, FOCUSED ON DEVELOPING ACADEMIC SKILLS AND COMPETENCIES INCLUDING: PROBLEM SOLVING, CRITICAL THINKING, AND SOCIAL RESPONSIBILITY. THE MIDDLE SCHOOL PROVIDES THE OPTIMAL LEARNING ENVIRONMENT WHERE STUDENTS ARE CHALLENGED ACADEMICALLY THROUGH OUR BALANCED PROGRAM THAT ENSURES HIGH SCHOOL READINESS WHILE INSPIRING STUDENTS TO STAY ENGAGED AND MAKE A DIFFERENCE IN THE WORLD. WE ALSO OFFER AFTER SCHOOL CARE, ENRICHMENT PROGRAMS AND SUMMER CAMPS. OVERALL ENROLLMENT IS APPROXIMATELY 140 STUDENTS.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: TEACHER CANDIDATES - FRIENDS SCHOOL OFFERS A UNIQUE TEACHER PREPARATION PROGRAM TO ASPIRING TEACHERS BY PROVIDING THEM WITH PRACTICAL CLASSROOM TEACHING EXPERIENCE AND COURSEWORK THAT ENABLES THEM TO ATTAIN A COLORADO INITIAL TEACHER LICENSE, AS WELL AS A MASTER'S DEGREE IN EDUCATION IN PARTNERSHIP WITH THE UNIVERSITY OF COLORADO DENVER. THIS HIGHLY REGARDED EDUCATION PROGRAM PLACES TEACHERS IN PUBLIC AND INDEPENDENT SCHOOLS THROUGHOUT BOULDER COUNTY AND SURROUNDING COUNTIES. WE LAUNCH APPROXIMATELY 16-24 TEACHER CANDIDATES EACH YEAR.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: CHAT'S MISSION IS TO NURTURE, EXPLORE AND ILLUMINATE THE HMONG AMERICAN EXPERIENCE THROUGH ARTISTIC EXPRESSION
MissionDesc: CHAT'S MISSION IS TO NURTURE, EXPLORE AND ILLUMINATE THE HMONG AMERICAN EXPERIENCE THROUGH ARTISTIC EXPRESSION
Desc: CENTER FOR HMONG ARTS AND TALENT HAD A BUSY 2024. STARTING IN MAY, CHAT ORGANIZED AN ASIAN AMERICAN FESTIVAL IN MICHIGAN TO CELEBRATE ASIAN AMERICAN HERITAGE MONTH IN MAY. CHAT HAS OVER 8,000 PEOPLE ATTEND THE EVENT WITH VIETNAMESE, LAO, THAI, CHINESE, EAST INDIAN, BENGALI, CAMBODIAN AND HMONG DANCERS AND ARTISTS SHARING THEIR CULTURE THROUGH DANCE, DRUM, MUSIC, AND SINGING. CHAT ALSO ORGANIZED THE INTERNATIONAL HMONG FASHION WITH DESIGNERS FROM LAOS, THAILAND AND VIETNAM IN THE FIRST QUARTER OF 2024. CHAT HELD THEIR ANNUAL FRESH TRADITIONS FASHION SHOW AT THE ST. PAUL RIVERCENTRE AGAIN WITH OVER 10 DESIGNERS FROM MINNESOTA, WISCONSIN AND CALIFORNIA. CHAT WAS ABLE TO SPONSOR THE LEGENDARY HMONG SINGER LUJ YAJ FROM THAILAND FOR A 5 MONTH SUCCESSFUL CONCERT TOUR. CHAT WAS ABLE TO HELP, ASSIST AND WORK WITH OVER 500 HMONG ARTISTS IN 2024 AND HAD A TOTAL AGGREGATE LIVE AUDIENCE OF 75,000 PEOPLE ATTEND THEIR LIVE EVENTS.

ActivityOrMissionDesc: The American Legion was chartered and incorporated by Congress in 1919 as a patriotic veterans organization devoted to mutual helpfulness. It is the nations largest wartime veterans service orgnanization, comited to mentoring you
MissionDesc: The American Legion was chartered and incorporated by Congress in 1919 as a patriotic veterans organization devoted to mutual helpfulness. It is the nations largest wartime veterans service orgnanization, comited to mentoring you
Desc: San Diegueto American Legion Post 416 600 members documented over 25,000 hours of community service to our active duty veterans and their families in San Diego County providing multiple visits to the VA Hospital, Balboa Naval medical Center and County Veterans Homes. Legions Baseball expanded in the state and specifically in Southern California due to the efforts of post 416. Held all standard events including Memorial Day, 4th of July, Veterans Day, Marine Corps birthday, educating the local community in civics, history and to more fully understand the sacrifices of their local neighbors and friends. Selected sponsored and supported 9 young men to attend Boys State.

Desc: WE PROVIDED PROGRAMMING TO EMPOWER YOUTH TO SELF-COMFORT AND DISCOVER PATHWAYS TO THEIR DREAMS. PROGRAMS INCLUDE YOGA, JOURNALING, MEDITATION EXERCISES AND PILLOWCASE DECORATING. ActivityOrMissionDesc: HELP MICHIGAN STUDENTS, K-12, GET BACK IN SCHOOL, STAY IN SCHOOL AND GROW (SEE SCH O)THE STUDENT ADVOCACY CENTER OF MICHIGAN WORKS COLLABORATIVELY WITH UNDERSERVED STUDENTS, AND THEIR FAMILIES, TO STAY IN SCHOOL, REALIZE THEIR RIGHTS TO A QUALITY PUBLIC EDUCATION, GROW AND EXPERIENCE SUCCESS.
MissionDesc: THE STUDENT ADVOCACY CENTER OF MICHIGAN WORKS COLLABORATIVELY WITH UNDERSERVED STUDENTS, AND THEIR FAMILIES, TO STAY IN SCHOOL, REALIZE THEIR RIGHTS TO A QUALITY PUBLIC EDUCATION, GROW AND EXPERIENCE SUCCESS.
Desc: EDUCATION ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT: SAC PROVIDES NO-COST ADVOCACY AND SUPPORT FOR FAMILIES IN EDUCATIONAL CRISIS (EXPULSION, SUSPENSION, SCHOOL FAILURE, ENROLLMENT DENIAL, DISCRIMINATION). WE ASSESS NEEDS, EXPLORE RIGHTS AND OPTIONS, BUILD SELF-ADVOCACY SKILLS, COORDINATE WITH THE SCHOOLS AND COCREATE PLANS THAT RESULT IN IMPROVED ATTENDANCE, BEHAVIOR AND GRADES. WE HAVE A STATEWIDE HELPLINE AND IN-PERSON SUPPORT IN WAYNE, WASHTENAW AND JACKSON COUNTIES.
ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp_Desc: CHECK & CONNECT IS AN INTENSIVE, STRUCTURED, LONG-TERM, AND EVIDENCE-BASED MENTORING PROGRAM FOR STUDENTS WHO ARE DISCONNECTED FROM SCHOOL AND LEARNING. THE PROGRAM, STARTED IN THE 1990S FROM THE UNIVERSITY OF MINNESOTA, HAS BEEN PROVEN TO INCREASE SCHOOL COMPLETION. SAC FIRST PILOTED CHECK & CONNECT IN 2012 IN YPSILANTI AND NOW PROVIDES THE PROGRAM IN YPSILANTI, ANN ARBOR, AND DETROIT. WE WORK WITH LOW- AND NO-INCOME STUDENTS IMPACTED BY TRAUMA, MENTAL ILLNESS, RACISM, AND BARRIERS SUCH AS HARSH SCHOOL DISCIPLINE, TRUANCY, AND SCHOOL FAILURE. MENTORS AND MENTEES WORK TOGETHER FOR A MINIMUM OF TWO YEARS. CONTINUED ON SCHEDULE OEVERY WEEK, MENTORS CONNECT WITH EACH YOUTH, CHECK SCHOOL ENGAGEMENT DATA, DESIGN PERSONALIZED INTERVENTIONS BASED ON THAT DATA, ENCOURAGE GOALSETTING AND PROBLEM-SOLVING, AND SERVE AS A BRIDGE BETWEEN THE STUDENT, SCHOOL, CAREGIVERS, AND COMMUNITY. MENTEES IMPROVE ATTENDANCE, GRADES, AND DISCIPLINE AND ARE MORE LIKELY TO GRADUATE.
ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp_Desc: YOUTH ORGANIZING/COMMUNITY ORGANIZING/POLICY: THE VISION OF SAC FROM THE START WAS TO BOTH PROVIDE MEANINGFUL "SOCIAL SERVICE" TYPE SUPPORT AND INDIVIDUAL CASE ADVOCACY - BUT ALSO TO BUILD POWER OF STUDENTS AND FAMILIIES AND TOGETHER TAKE COLLECTIVE ACTION TO WIN CONCRETE CHANGES TO POLICIES AND PRACTICES THAT IMPROVE THE LIVES OF MANY MORE STUDENTS. SAC IS FOCUSED ON CONCRETE WINS THAT INCREASE STUDENT VOICE, CHOICE, SUPPORT AND CARE AND DECREASE HARSH DISCIPLINE AND CARCERAL SYSTEMS. AS PART OF THIS WORK, SAC ENGAGES IN YOUTH ORGANIZING IN A PROGRAM CALLED YOUTH ACTION MICHIGAN (YAM) IN DETROIT AND YPSILANTI. YAM IS AN EDUCATION JUSTICE YOUTH ORGANIZING PROGRAM THAT WORKS TO BUILD YOUTH LEADERSHIP, POWER AND BETTER SCHOOLS. WE COMBAT SCHOOL PUSH-OUT AND WORK TOWARDS HEALTHY AND HEALING SCHOOLS. CONTINUED ON SCHEDULE O SAC ALSO ENGAGES CAREGIVERS AND COMMUNITY MEMBERS IN ORGANIZING LOCALLY AND IN STATEWIDE WORK.
